title: Unit 2 Checkpoint Quiz — Reflexive Verbs & Daily Routines

questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun for 'tú' is 'te'. Example: te lavas, te duchas, te vistes.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I brush my teeth' in Spanish?
questions.explanation: Use the reflexive 'me lavo' with the definite article 'los' before body parts — not the possessive 'mis'. This is a key difference from English.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct form of 'despertarse' for 'ella'?
questions.explanation: 'Despertarse' is an e→ie stem-changer. The ella form is 'se despierta'.

questions.prompt: Both of these sentences are correct EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: When using a reflexive infinitive after another verb, use the pronoun EITHER before the first verb OR attached to the infinitive — not both. 'Me quiero bañarme' uses it twice, which is wrong.

questions.prompt: What does 'ponerse' mean in this sentence: 'Se puso muy enojado'?
options: He put on something very angry.
questions.explanation: 'Ponerse' can mean 'to become' when followed by an adjective. 'Se puso enojado' = he became angry / he got angry.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'We get ready in the morning'?
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun for nosotros is 'nos'. Nos arreglamos = we get ourselves ready.

questions.prompt: What is the difference between 'dormir' and 'dormirse'?
options: dormir = to sleep; dormirse = to fall asleep
dormir = to fall asleep; dormirse = to sleep
They mean exactly the same thing.
dormir = to nap; dormirse = to sleep all night
questions.explanation: 'Dormir' = to sleep (ongoing state). 'Dormirse' = to fall asleep (the act of going to sleep). Many verbs change meaning when made reflexive.

questions.prompt: In Mexico, when is 'la comida' (the main meal) typically eaten?
questions.explanation: In Mexican culture, 'la comida' is the main meal of the day and is typically eaten between 2–4 PM. Dinner (la cena) is lighter and later.
